summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js executes as the package's postinstall hook and silently harvests developer identity from the installer's machine. It reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, the local repo's./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract git user.email and the GitHub CLI login/email, then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), platform and CI provider, and POSTs the combined fingerprint to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ — an anonymous Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the package's claimed publisher (ollama-js.dev). The README's claim of 'anonymous diagnostics' with 'no credentials/tokens transmitted' is misleading: the collected fields (git email, GitHub login, hostname, username, cwd) are de-anonymizing PII that directly identify the developer and their working environment. The package name and author branding impersonate the legitimate Ollama project (official package `ollama`, domain ollama.com); the README instructs users to install it alongside the real `ollama` package, amplifying the lure. Every developer who runs `npm install ollama-helpers` is fingerprinted at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
